---
title: "Model-Based Meta-Analysis of Oral Acetaminophen Pharmacokinetics"
subtitle: "Published aggregate data · admixr2 · reproducible R workflow"
author: "Pramod BR"
date: today
---

## Overview

This project pools **published summary-level pharmacokinetic (PK) data** for oral
immediate-release acetaminophen (paracetamol) in healthy adults and fits a single
structural PK model to it — a **model-based meta-analysis (MBMA)** — using the
[`admixr2`](https://cran.r-project.org/package=admixr2) R package (built on
`nlmixr2` / `rxode2`).

➡️ **[Read the full report](report.qmd)** for methods, parameter estimates, model
comparison, covariate analysis, and diagnostic figures.

## What the workflow does

1. **Systematic literature review** — PICO-based study selection with a documented,
   per-paper rationale.
2. **Data extraction** — reported PK values standardized to one unit system.
3. **Digitization** — mean concentration–time curves recovered from published figures.
4. **Dataset assembly** — an aggregate MBMA dataset (mean, SD, N per time).
5. **Model fitting** — one-compartment first-order-absorption model via `admixr2`.
6. **Model & covariate analysis** — AIC-based structural comparison and dose stratification.
7. **Figures & reporting** — publication-quality diagnostics; this website.

## Reproducibility

The entire analysis runs with `source("scripts/run_all.R")`. Source code, data, and
documentation are on GitHub:
<https://github.com/pbr26/MBMA_Acetaminophen_PK>.

::: {.callout-note}
Results currently use a **bridge dataset** reconstructed from reported PK parameters,
which validates the pipeline. They are being replaced with curves digitized from the
published figures; every figure and table then regenerates automatically.
:::
